bindings.quest_action: explore the uncharted ruins and retrieve what's inside
bindings.quest_focus: an ancient ruin no one has entered in generations
bindings.quest_stakes: a discovery that could change our understanding of the old world
bindings.quest_reward_hint: whatever treasures the ruins still hold

dialogueChunks.intro: Ah, {player_name}! You look like someone who doesn't shy away from the unknown. Perfect timing.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: I've been mapping routes through the wilds for months, and I've found something remarkable: {quest_focus} {location_hint}. My scouts report the area is thick with {enemy_type}, but the carvings on the entrance suggest there's {quest_item} inside. I need someone with real steel in their spine to {quest_action}.
dialogueChunks.outro: What do you say? Fortune favors the bold, and I have a feeling you're exactly the kind of bold we need.

playerResponses.text: Uncharted ruins? I was born for this.
Sounds dangerous. What's my cut?
Find someone else. I'm not interested in suicide missions.

bindings.quest_action: recover the rare materials for an extraordinary weapon
bindings.quest_focus: a deep ore vein lost to time
bindings.quest_stakes: the chance to forge something that hasn't been made in centuries
bindings.quest_threat: the creatures nesting around the deposit
bindings.quest_reward_hint: a piece of history worthy of legend

dialogueChunks.intro: Hold on, don't go yet! I've been waiting weeks for someone capable to walk through that door, and here you are, {player_name}.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: I'm working on something extraordinary: {quest_stakes}. The old techniques are lost, but the materials aren't. I need {quest_item} from {quest_focus} deep in the caves {location_hint}. The problem? The {enemy_type} have nested all around it. Clear the way and bring me what I need, and I'll craft {quest_reward_hint}.
dialogueChunks.outro: This isn't charity work: you'll walk away with a piece of history. So, are you in?

playerResponses.text: A legendary weapon? Count me in.
I'll get your materials, but I want first pick of what you forge.
I don't fight monsters for a blacksmith's pet project.

bindings.quest_action: take down the warlord commanding the raiders
bindings.quest_stakes: the safety of the trade routes
bindings.quest_threat: a warlord who's been terrorizing the region
bindings.quest_reward_hint: a serious bounty from the merchants' guild

dialogueChunks.intro: Well met, {player_name}. I'll skip the pleasantries: I have a proposition that could make us both very wealthy.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_threat} has been terrorizing {quest_stakes} near {location}. The merchants' guild is offering {quest_reward_hint} for their head. {target_npc} tried to collect last month and barely made it back alive. {quest_focus} is {location_hint}, heavily fortified. But I've studied their patrols and I know there's a gap in their defenses just before dawn.
dialogueChunks.outro: One strike, one kill, and we split the bounty. You handle the fighting, I handle the intel. Deal?

playerResponses.text: Show me the patrol routes. I'll handle the rest.
Sixty-forty my way, since I'm the one risking my neck.
Too risky. Find another sword for hire.

bindings.quest_action: clear the mountain passes and recover the stockpiled caches
bindings.quest_focus: a lost trade route through the mountains
bindings.quest_stakes: control of the most valuable trade corridor in the region
bindings.quest_threat: the creatures that sealed the passes generations ago
bindings.quest_origin: a merchant's journal from two centuries ago

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}! Exactly who I was looking for. I've uncovered something that's going to change everything.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Deep in the archives, I found {quest_origin} describing {quest_focus} that was abandoned after a {enemy_type} incursion. The journal mentions caches of {quest_item} stockpiled at waypoints along the route. Nobody has touched them in generations. If we can {quest_action}, we'd control {quest_stakes}.
dialogueChunks.outro: This is bigger than one haul, {player_name}. This is a legacy. And you'd be the one who made it possible.

playerResponses.text: A lost trade route with untouched stockpiles? Say no more.
Sounds profitable. What percentage are we talking?
Two-hundred-year-old maps and sealed passes? That's a fool's errand.

bindings.quest_action: raid the grove and harvest the bounty before the pack returns
bindings.quest_focus: a hidden grove overflowing with rare resources
bindings.quest_stakes: a haul that would set us up for months
bindings.quest_threat: a massive pack that controls the territory
bindings.quest_origin: a near-death scouting run last month

dialogueChunks.intro: Have a seat, {player_name}. What I'm about to show you is going to make your jaw drop.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: See this map? {target_npc} drew it from memory after stumbling out of the deep forest half-dead last month. There's {quest_focus} {location_hint} where {quest_item} grows wild and untouched. The catch? The grove is the territory of {quest_threat} of {enemy_type}. {target_npc} barely escaped. But I've been studying their behavior for years, and I think I've found a patrol window.
dialogueChunks.outro: One precise strike, in and out before they know what happened. {quest_stakes}. Interested?

playerResponses.text: A precision raid into monster territory? That's my kind of enterprise.
If {target_npc} almost died, I want hazard pay.
If your expert barely survived, what chance do I have? Pass.

bindings.quest_action: reach the sealed vault at the heart of the proving grounds
bindings.quest_focus: the old proving grounds and their deepest vault
bindings.quest_stakes: being the first person ever to breach the vault
bindings.quest_threat: traps and guardians that have stopped every challenger
bindings.quest_reward_hint: artifacts of a quality found nowhere else

dialogueChunks.intro: You look like someone who appreciates a challenge, {player_name}. Am I right? Because I've got the challenge of a lifetime.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_focus} {location_hint} were built centuries ago to test warriors. According to {target_npc}, the deepest chamber holds a vault that hasn't been opened since the original builders sealed it. Inside? {quest_item} of unmatched quality. The problem is {quest_threat}: traps, guardians, {enemy_type}, the works. Every adventurer who's tried has turned back at the third hall.
dialogueChunks.outro: Nobody has ever reached the vault. Think about that: nobody. {quest_stakes}. Your name carved into history.

playerResponses.text: The first to reach the vault? That's a record worth setting.
Ancient traps and guardians sound expensive to survive. Make it worth my while.
There's a reason nobody has reached it. I'll keep my head attached, thanks.

bindings.quest_action: chart the uncharted wilderness and claim its resources
bindings.quest_focus: a stretch of wilderness no cartographer has ever mapped
bindings.quest_stakes: opening territory nobody else has dared to touch
bindings.quest_threat: the hostile creatures that own the land
bindings.quest_reward_hint: resources in quantities that would make a merchant weep

dialogueChunks.intro: Perfect timing, {player_name}. I've been looking for a partner, and everyone else thinks I'm crazy.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: I want to {quest_action}. There's {quest_focus} {location_hint}. Why uncharted? Because the {enemy_type} own it. But I've spent months interviewing hunters and trappers, and I know there's {quest_item} out there in quantities worth the risk. {target_npc} offered to bankroll the expedition if someone with real combat ability leads the way.
dialogueChunks.outro: We map the land, we claim {quest_reward_hint}, and we open up {quest_stakes}. Everyone wins.

playerResponses.text: Uncharted territory and unclaimed riches? Let's make history.
I'll lead the way, but I get first pick of whatever we find.
There's a reason it's uncharted. I like living too much.

bindings.quest_action: break into the sealed crypt and retrieve the prize
bindings.quest_focus: a sealed crypt that's been locked for centuries
bindings.quest_stakes: an absurd sum from a collector who pays on delivery
bindings.quest_threat: the creatures that have turned the crypt into a fortress

dialogueChunks.intro: Ah, {player_name}. I was beginning to think I'd have to do this alone. Glad that won't be necessary.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: A collector is offering {quest_stakes} for a specific piece: {quest_item} from {quest_focus} {location_hint}. {target_npc} and I have been researching it for months. The real obstacle isn't the locks or the walls: it's the {enemy_type} that have taken up residence inside. {quest_threat} means fighting through them first.
dialogueChunks.outro: {quest_reward_hint}, and a story nobody will believe. What do you say?

playerResponses.text: A sealed crypt, dangerous monsters, and a fortune waiting at the end? I'm in.
How absurd is the sum, exactly? I need numbers before I risk my neck.
Crypts are sealed for a reason. Count me out.
